EUR.EUR/USD: The euro is under pressure as European Central Bank officials debate doubling banks' minimum reserve requirement to 2% from 1%, according to a report. EUR/USD is facing resistance at 1.1450 against the US dollar. The ECB is expected to sustain a hawkish bias with one more hike, according to ING
EUR.EUR/GBP: The euro is seeing moderate gains against the pound, with EUR/GBP trading slightly higher. The European Central Bank's debate on doubling banks' minimum reserve requirement is being watched, while the BoE's stance remains steady. The EUR COT LF short -67.5k reflects a bearish positioning
EUR.EUR/JPY: The euro is trading higher against the yen, with EUR/JPY at 163.41. The ECB's potential doubling of banks' minimum reserve requirement is being weighed against the BoJ's steady stance. JPY COT LF short -115,033 reflects crowded carry positioning
EUR.EUR/AUD: The euro is steady against the Australian dollar, with EUR/AUD trading flat. The RBA's hawkish minutes met firmer US confidence data, supporting AUD. However, the ECB's debate on reserve requirements is being watched
EUR.EUR/CAD: The euro is gaining against the Canadian dollar, with EUR/CAD trading higher. Canada's GDP growth topped forecasts, but May guidance remains soft, limiting CAD recovery. CAD COT LF short -87,096 shows institutional bearishness
EUR.EUR/CHF: The euro is steady against the Swiss franc, with EUR/CHF trading flat. The Swiss KOF Barometer climbed above the long-term average, improving the economic outlook. The SNB is on hold
EUR.EUR/NZD: The euro is trading lower against the New Zealand dollar, with EUR/NZD at 1.7683. NZD leads G10 performance, supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias and a +335bp OIS carry advantage over JPY

GBP.GBP/USD: The British pound is steady after Bank of England Governor Bailey stated that the BoE has time to judge the pass-through of higher energy prices. The UK economy grew by 0.6% at the start of the year. The BoE is on hold, with no change in policy expected in 2026
GBP.GBP/JPY: The pound is trading higher against the yen, with GBP/JPY at 163.11. BOJ policymaker Sato stated that short-term volatile moves in the exchange rate are undesirable. JPY COT LF short -115,033 reflects crowded carry positioning
